elements.name: epsilon
elements.description: spin involution: kernel of Spin(8) -> SO(8)

outer_automorphism_group.name: S_3
outer_automorphism_group.note: Spin(8) is the unique compact simple Lie group with |Out| > 2.

central_action_on_8dim_irreducibles.description: Action of each central element on the three 8-dim irreducibles V, S+, S-. Signs indicate trivial (+) or by -I (-) action.

quotients_by_central_subgroups.name: SO(8)
quotients_by_central_subgroups.notes: standard SO(8); kernel is the spin involution

quotients_by_central_subgroups.name: PSO(8)
quotients_by_central_subgroups.notes: trivial center; no Z/6 in pi_1

structural_obstruction.name: no_cyclic_Z_6_in_center_or_pi_1
structural_obstruction.description: Spin(8)'s center is Z/2 x Z/2 (Klein four-group), which has no element of order 3 or 6. Quotients by central subgroups have pi_1 in {trivial, Z/2, Z/2 x Z/2}; none have cyclic Z/6. The T2' template, which requires pi_1 containing a cyclic group of the monodromy order (6 for D_4), fails for Spin(8) and its quotients.
structural_obstruction.consequence: Three resolution strategies (A non-simple extension, B non-scalar auxiliary, C graded polarization) considered; no scalar-central-element resolution is possible.
structural_obstruction.invalid_claim_rejected: Spin(8) center is cyclic of order 6 (false; see fixture d4-c7-prime-scoping.false-cyclic-center.invalid.json)

representation_catalog.description: Spin(8) irreducible complex representations of dimension <= 350.

dimension_6_question.rationale: Smallest non-trivial irreducible has dim 8; a faithful 6-dim rep would have to be a direct sum of smaller irreducibles, but the only irreducibles of dim <= 6 are the 1-dim trivial rep. A direct sum of trivials is not faithful.
dimension_6_question.consequence: The T2'_{A_n} dimensional pattern V_{A_n} = C^{n+1} = C^{h(A_n)} does not extend to D_4 with V_{D_4} = C^6.

strategies.label: A
strategies.name: non_simple_extension
strategies.construction: Z_diag = <(epsilon, e^{pi*i/3})> as a Z/6 subgroup of Z(Spin(8)) x U(1)
strategies.verdict: Resolves pi_1 obstruction, but no 6-dim faithful rep. Polarization remains 8-dim.

strategies.label: B
strategies.name: non_scalar_auxiliary_element
strategies.construction: Stokes multiplier acts as a non-scalar order-6 element M_6 in Spin(8), not a central scalar.

viable_polarization_candidates.stokes_multiplier_eigenvalues: {e^{i*pi/3}, e^{-i*pi/3}, e^{i*theta_2}, e^{-i*theta_2}, e^{i*theta_3}, e^{-i*theta_3}, 1, 1} for chosen theta_2, theta_3

harness_modifications_required: P1: scalar -> matrix-conjugacy check on the chosen 8-dim rep
P5: zeta_central -> M_6 commutes with chosen subgroup (centralizer check)
strategies.verdict: Cleanest representation-theoretically; harness contract changes are bounded and well-defined.

strategies.label: C
strategies.name: graded_decomposable_polarization
strategies.G_spatial: Spin(8) x H for some auxiliary group H
strategies.construction: V_{D_4} = V_1 oplus V_2 with monodromy acting on V_1 with order a, on V_2 with order b, lcm(a,b)=6.
strategies.form_type: mixed (orthogonal on V_1, depends on H on V_2)

viable_decompositions.V_2_source: SU(3) embedded in SO(6) subset Spin(8)
Spin(8) 8-dim rep with order-6 element acting

strategies.verdict: Graded decomposition possible but ad hoc; natural dimensions do not align with h(D_4)=6 cleanly.

comparative_assessment.rationale: Strategy B is cleanest representation-theoretically: it keeps Spin(8) as spatial group and a single 8-dim faithful representation as the polarization carrier. The harness contract changes from scalar checks to matrix checks, but those changes are bounded and well-defined.
deferred_questions: extension to higher D_n and E_n

next_concrete_deliverable.description: D_4 scoping under Strategy B: commit to Spin(8) as spatial group with chosen 8-dim irreducible as polarization space and explicit order-6 element M_6 as non-scalar Stokes multiplier.
